recipe.title: Small Batch Baked Ube Mochi Donuts

ingredients.name: purple food coloring
ingredients.note: optional but used in source photos for deep violet

ingredients.name: whole milk
ingredients.quantity: ½ cup + 1 tbsp batter; 2 tsp glaze

steps.text: Preheat oven to 350°F; position rack center.
steps.visualCue: oven already open in Licorn kitchen, steady amber glow
steps.runwayRisk: avoid oven door manipulation in same shot as tray slide

steps.text: Whisk mochiko, baking powder, and salt in a small bowl.
steps.visualCue: fine white rice flour mound with purple streaks about to enter
steps.textureCue: powdery matte flour vs glossy wet base
steps.runwayRisk: flour cloud explosion if whisked too violently on camera

steps.text: Whisk melted butter, sugar, and egg yolks until combined; mixture may look lumpy then smooth.
steps.visualCue: pale yellow glossy base turning satin as yolks emulsify
steps.runwayRisk: lumpy-to-smooth transition may morph into scrambled texture

steps.text: Add ube jam, ube extract, and purple coloring; whisk until violet.
steps.visualCue: jam streaks marble then homogenize to deep lilac
steps.textureCue: color bloom from magenta jam pockets
steps.runwayRisk: color may drift pink or blue without extract lock

steps.text: Gradually whisk in milk until thin pourable batter.
steps.visualCue: liquid violet stream folding into batter
steps.runwayRisk: batter may read as cake batter or pancake mix without mochi sheen

steps.text: Fold dry ingredients into wet until just combined; transfer to liquid measuring cup with spout.
steps.visualCue: thick violet batter lifted on whisk, last flour pockets vanish
steps.runwayRisk: over-mixing looks like glue; under-mixed white streaks

steps.text: Lightly spray metal donut pan cavities; pour batter to brim of each cavity.
steps.visualCue: six rings filling flush, no overflow because minimal rise
steps.textureCue: batter meniscus at pan lip, fine spray mist
steps.runwayRisk: ring count drift, oversized rings, batter overflow fantasy rise

steps.text: Bake 35 minutes until tops spring back and skewer shows moist crumbs.
steps.visualCue: matte violet rings with lightly cracked tops, flat bottoms
steps.textureCue: crispy shell set, faint steam on exit
steps.runwayRisk: donuts may puff into cake domes; must keep flat-bottom mochi profile

steps.text: Cool in pan 5 minutes, turn out onto wire rack to cool completely.
steps.visualCue: six rings on rack, flat bases visible, springy tops
steps.runwayRisk: rings sticking or tearing on unmold

steps.text: Whisk ube jam and milk into glaze; add sifted sugar in batches; finish with extract and remaining milk.
steps.visualCue: glossy lavender glaze ribbon dripping off whisk
steps.textureCue: thick then loosened pour, satin sheen
steps.runwayRisk: glaze too thin (drip flood) or too thick (frosting)

steps.text: Dip donut tops in glaze, flip upright on rack; garnish with sprinkles immediately.
steps.visualCue: half-dipped crown gloss, sprinkle bounce on wet glaze
steps.textureCue: dip pull, glaze curtain, sprinkle tick sounds
steps.runwayRisk: donut geometry collapse during dip; sprinkles as confetti storm

steps.text: Serve same day; glaze sets 1–2 hours optional.
steps.visualCue: six glazed rings on terrazzo island, mascot satisfied nearby
steps.textureCue: set glaze shell vs chewy interior implied
steps.runwayRisk: character touching dish in outro-adjacent shots

assumptions: Standard Wilton metal 6-cavity donut pan (~3 in / 7.5–8 cm outer ring diameter, ~1 in / 2.5 cm center hole) as referenced by the source author.
Exactly six donuts on rack for hero shots to match small-batch yield.
Festive mixed sprinkles unless operator specifies otherwise.
Licorn kitchen uses pre-open oven wide shot; no on-camera oven door hinge action.
Purple tone: deep violet-lilac (ube jam + extract + Americolor-style purple), not neon pink.

timing.cook: 40 minutes (35 min bake + 5 min pan cool)

criticalTransformations: Lumpy butter-sugar-egg base emulsifies smooth after yolks
Ube jam and extract bloom batter from pale yellow to homogenous deep violet
Thin pourable mochi batter gains slight elasticity when dry rice flour is folded in
Liquid batter filled to pan brim sets into flat-bottom baked rings with minimal vertical rise
Baked shell goes from steamy matte crackle to cool springy bounce
Thick ube glaze loosens to dip consistency then sets glossy on crown
Dry ring becomes glazed crown with immediate sprinkle adhesion
visualTextureOpportunities: Violet batter stream from spouted cup into metal ring cavity (opening hook)
Whisk blade cutting glossy lilac batter with elastic tail
Dry mochiko flour avalanche into wet violet (contrast)
Fine cooking spray mist on metal cavities before pour
Batter meniscus flush with pan lip across six rings
Skewer pull showing moist mochi crumbs (not dry cake)
Gentle finger press bounce on baked top
Glaze dip with upside-down flip and dripping curtain
Sprinkle rain hitting wet glaze (ASMR ticks)
Crispy exterior vs chewy interior implied on tear-free cross-section avoidance (keep rings intact for outro lock)
possibleHooks: Macro pour: viscous violet mochi batter fills one donut ring to the brim
Color bloom: ube jam streaks marble through yellow base then snap to solid lilac
Hard cut: pale raw-filled pan to matte cracked purple baked rings in oven
Glaze dip: crown submerged then lifted with glossy curtain and sprinkle burst

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the garnish sprinkles be rainbow confetti mix, single-color jimmies, or pearl/neutral sprinkles for the hero and reference stills?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Sprinkle color contrast changes the final dish identity clause and GPT-Image reference for FinalDishVisual.
